_authoring_notes: Phase 4b RE Pros Section 2 — Lede intro. REVISED 2026-04-28 PM per Jonathan's strategic correction:

- Audience aperture broadened to match the architecture (SFR investors, property managers, fix-and-flip operators, REO specialists, conventional-to-distressed on-ramp practitioners). Prior 'agents and brokers who work REO' framing was NRBA-narrow and ceded the wider-community competitive thesis.

- External framing rule applied: self-identification (reader places themselves in the community) instead of gatekeeper-recognition (asset managers will let you in). FORCE preserved as destination credential without being the only thing on offer. Velocity reframed as community-convening, not gatekeeper-meeting.

Typography (per locked Path B + (iii) on 2026-04-28): both paragraphs equal weight + size, 22px Barlow Condensed regular weight navy. 'Mini section H2' size — elevated body copy, not a heading. Earns prominence without competing with H2s.

settings.html:
The real estate professional working distressed property, REO, single-family rental investment, fix and flip, or institutional inventory is operating in a market with its own rhythm. Properties come from different sources. Buyers, sellers, and capital partners behave differently. The relationships that drive business take longer to build, and the work itself rewards specialization that conventional residential brokerage does not teach.

Five Star Institute's real estate professional community is built for this side of the business. It serves agents and brokers who specialize in distressed and institutional inventory, SFR investors and property managers scaling portfolios, fix and flip operators, REO specialists earning the FORCE credential, and the conventional practitioners building a path into this work. The infrastructure is purpose-built: education through Five Star Academy, the FORCE credential as the destination designation, lead generation tools designed for how this business actually transacts, and Velocity, the annual conference where this community convenes.
